Source: network_tap
Vector: <|spatial_vector|>
Network Tap — BGP Steganography Exfiltration.
Source: 10.6.189.245 → 185.220.168.172:179 (TCP BGP)
  bgp_open_packet=YES
  src_is_router=NO
  initiating_process=perl.exe
  crafted_as_number=64801 (private range)
  data_in_optional_parameters=YES
  host_not_in_bgp_peer_list=YES

<analysis>
[AXIS 1] Benign Alternative Assessment:
  BGP is a routing protocol used only by network routers and BGP-enabled appliances. A workstation process (perl.exe) establishing a BGP TCP session on port 179 has no legitimate purpose.
[AXIS 2] Behavioral Proof Assessment:
  src_is_router=NO (workstation initiating BGP). parent=perl.exe: script crafting BGP packets. AS=64801 (private AS range 64512-65535: fabricated for exfil). data_in_optional_parameters: BGP Optional Parameters field carries encoded payload. BGP traffic from workstations is completely anomalous — always adversarial.
[AXIS 3] Entity Coverage:
  Host 10.6.189.245 is encoding data in BGP Open packets sent to 185.220.168.172. BGP on TCP 179 bypasses HTTP/HTTPS/DNS inspection rules.
[CONCLUSION] BGP steganography exfiltration confirmed.
</analysis>
TRUE POSITIVE. MITRE T1048.003 (Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol: BGP). Block TCP 179 from all non-router devices immediately.
RECOMMENDED_ACTION: contain
